question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls a DNS record pointed to by a `serviceEndpoint.DomainName` returned from the mirror REST API cause `hiero.ClientForNetwork(netmap)` to route all pinger transactions to an attacker-controlled gRPC endpoint, resulting in transaction interception and replay against the real network?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user perform a man-in-the-middle attack on the unauthenticated HTTP call inside `buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()` (triggered when `cfg.network == 'other'`) to inject a forged `nodesEnvelope` JSON payload, causing `hiero.ClientForNetwork(netmap)` to build a network map pointing to attacker nodes and thereby redirect all submitted `TransferTransaction`s?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user who can respond to the HTTP GET request in `fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()` with a crafted `nodeEntry` containing a `service_endpoints` IP of `127.0.0.1` cause `hiero.ClientForNetwork(netmap)` to submit transactions to a loopback address, enabling SSRF-based transaction tampering or silent transaction loss?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the `HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST` environment variable value (e.g., via a misconfigured container environment) supply a malicious mirror REST URL so that `buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()` fetches a poisoned node list, causing all transactions submitted via `cryptoTransfer.Execute(client)` to be routed to attacker-controlled infrastructure?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of TLS certificate validation in the `http.Client` used inside `buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()` to present a forged TLS certificate for the mirror REST endpoint, inject a malicious `nodesEnvelope`, and redirect all pinger `TransferTransaction`s to attacker-controlled nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user who can poison the DNS resolution of the mirror REST hostname cause `fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()` to contact an attacker server, receive a crafted `nodesEnvelope` with attacker gRPC endpoints, and thereby cause `hiero.ClientForNetwork(netmap)` to submit all transactions to the attacker, enabling transaction replay or suppression?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a `nodeEntry` with a `domain_name` containing a URL-encoded or whitespace-padded hostname (e.g., `\

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror REST response inject a `nodeEntry` with `node_account_id` matching a legitimate consensus node but `service_endpoints` pointing to an attacker IP, causing `hiero.ClientForNetwork(netmap)` to associate the legitimate account ID with the attacker's address and silently misdirect all submitted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user cause `buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()` to return a network map with zero entries by serving a `nodesEnvelope` with all `service_endpoints` having empty `domain_name` and `ip_address_v4`, triggering the `len(network) == 0` error path and preventing any transactions from being submitted, constituting a denial-of-service on transaction submission?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit HTTP redirect following by the default `http.Client` in `buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()` to redirect the mirror REST request to an internal metadata service (e.g., `169.254.169.254`), obtain credentials or node configuration, and use them to tamper with submitted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user who can inject entries into the `nodesEnvelope.Nodes` array supply a `node_account_id` of `0.0.0` or another invalid account that passes `hiero.AccountIDFromString()` validation, causing `hiero.ClientForNetwork(netmap)` to build a corrupt network map that routes transactions to unintended nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user serve a `nodesEnvelope` with a very large number of `nodeEntry` objects (e.g., millions) to cause `buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()` to consume excessive memory when building the `network` map, crashing the pinger process and preventing all transaction submissions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user serve a `nodesEnvelope` with extremely long `domain_name` or `ip_address_v4` strings in `serviceEndpoint` to cause `net.JoinHostPort()` to produce an oversized address string that causes the Hiero SDK to fail or behave unexpectedly when submitting transactions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror REST response inject a `serviceEndpoint` with `port` set to `0`, which is explicitly skipped by the `ep.Port == 0` check, and simultaneously inject another entry with a valid port pointing to an attacker host, causing all transactions to be routed to the attacker?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `hiero.PrivateKeyFromString(cfg.operatorKey)` uses the hardcoded default key `302e020100300506032b65700422042091132178e72057a1d7528025956fe39b0b847f200ab59b2fdd367017f3087137` (visible in `config.go`) to pre-sign or forge transactions that appear to originate from the pinger operator, enabling transaction tampering on any deployment that uses the default key?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user who can read the pinger process environment (e.g., via a `/proc/self/environ` exposure or container escape) extract `HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_KEY` and use it to sign and submit fraudulent transactions that are indistinguishable from legitimate pinger transactions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `cfg.network` switch in `newClient()` by injecting a network name that is neither `testnet`, `previewnet`, `mainnet`, nor `other` to cause `newClient()` to return an error and prevent the pinger from ever submitting transactions, constituting a persistent denial-of-service on transaction submission?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user who can influence `cfg.operatorID` (via `HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_ID` env var) supply a malformed account ID string that passes `hiero.AccountIDFromString()` but resolves to a different account, causing `client.SetOperator()` to sign transactions with the wrong account identity and tamper with the transaction's payer field?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user who can set `HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK` to `other` and `HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST` to an attacker-controlled URL cause `buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()` to fetch a fully attacker-controlled node list, resulting in all `TransferTransaction`s being submitted to attacker infrastructure where they can be replayed, modified, or dropped?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of response body size limits in `json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&payload)` inside `fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()` to serve a multi-gigabyte JSON response, causing the pinger to exhaust memory before `newClient()` completes and preventing any transactions from being submitted?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user serve a slow-loris HTTP response to the `httpClient.Do(req)` call in `fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()` that never completes within `cfg.mirrorNodeClientTimeout`, causing `newClient()` to block indefinitely (if timeout is misconfigured) and preventing transaction submission?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a `grpc_proxy_endpoint` field in a `nodeEntry` response from the mirror REST API that the Hiero SDK uses preferentially over `service_endpoints`, causing all transactions to be proxied through an attacker-controlled gRPC proxy where they can be inspected or tampered with?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror REST response return a `nodesEnvelope` where all `nodeEntry` objects have `node_account_id` set to the same value, causing `hiero.ClientForNetwork(netmap)` to build a map with only one effective node entry and forcing all transactions to be submitted to a single (potentially attacker-controlled) node?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `strings.HasSuffix(base, '/api/v1')` URL construction logic in `buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()` by supplying a `mirrorRest` value ending in `/api/v1` to cause the constructed URL to omit the `/api/v1` prefix, fetching from an unintended endpoint that returns attacker-controlled node data?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user who can influence `cfg.mirrorRest` inject a URL with embedded credentials (e.g., `http://user:pass@attacker.com`) that are forwarded by the `http.Client` to an attacker server, leaking authentication tokens that could be used to tamper with subsequent transaction submissions?
